title: Epic: Establish canonical Wave resumption docs and Beads backlog
description:
## Issue
The repository accumulated several planning and review documents during the modernization and Wiab import work. Some of them are now stale or contradictory, which makes resuming work slower than it should be.

## Goal
Create one canonical resume document, retire stale docs, initialize repo-local Beads tracking, and record the next-phase roadmap in epics and tasks.

## Plan
1. Add a canonical current-state document.
2. Update README and the surviving detailed ledgers to point at the canonical docs.
3. Remove stale one-off documents once their findings are folded into the canonical set.
4. Initialize repo-local Beads tracking in no-db mode and create epics/tasks for the remaining work.

## DoD
- `docs/current-state.md` exists and is the canonical resume document.
- README points to the canonical doc set.
- Stale planning/review documents are removed or clearly superseded.
- `.beads/issues.jsonl` contains the epics and tasks for the remaining work.
acceptance_criteria:
- Canonical doc set is easy to discover from `README.md`.
- Repo-local Beads backlog exists and is git-tracked.
- No stale review-only or superseded planning docs remain in the main docs surface.
close_reason: Canonical docs, Beads backlog, and stale-doc cleanup completed

title: Epic: Keep canonical Wave docs and Beads synchronized
description:
## Issue
The repository has one canonical resume guide now, but the remaining long-form ledgers still need to be kept in sync with the live Beads backlog so future work does not restart from stale assumptions.

## Goal
Keep the canonical docs map aligned with the live backlog, retire one-off planning artifacts, and document the current workspace conventions so the next agent does not need to rediscover them.

## Plan
1. Refresh the canonical docs map in README and the resume guide.
2. Retire or reclassify stale long-form docs as historical references.
3. Record the shared worktree root preference and keep Beads epics/tasks aligned with the docs surface.

## DoD
- README and docs/current-state.md point at the same canonical doc set.
- Historical ledgers are explicitly labeled as references, not source of truth.
- One-off planning artifacts are removed once their content is folded into the canonical docs.
- The worktree root preference is recorded in the repo instructions.
acceptance_criteria:
- The docs map is consistent across README, docs/current-state.md, and docs/epics/README.md.
- Stale one-off plan docs are removed or clearly marked historical.
- The repository instructions capture the shared worktree root preference.
- Beads remains the live task tracker for all remaining roadmap work.
close_reason: Completed by the 2026-03-18 docs maintenance refresh

title: Refresh the canonical docs map and README
description: Update README, docs/current-state.md, and docs/epics/README.md so they point at the same canonical starting point and live backlog.
close_reason: Completed in the docs maintenance refresh

title: Retire stale one-off docs and mark ledgers historical
description: Delete the resumption plan artifact and add historical status banners to the detailed ledgers that remain useful as references.
close_reason: Completed in the docs maintenance refresh

title: Record the shared worktree root preference
description: Update the repo instructions so agents use <user_worktree_root> for isolated worktrees and keep Beads aligned with the docs refresh.
close_reason: Completed in the docs maintenance refresh

title: Create canonical current-state and roadmap docs
description: Create the canonical resume document and link it from README and the remaining detailed plans.
close_reason: Completed as part of the 2026-03-18 documentation refresh

title: Initialize repo-local Beads tracking and capture the roadmap
description: Initialize `.beads/` in repo-local no-db mode and create the epics/tasks that reflect the remaining modernization and Wiab import work.
close_reason: Completed as part of the 2026-03-18 documentation refresh

title: Epic: Complete modernization phases 6 through 8
description:
## Issue
The Wave codebase already completed the Java 17, Gradle 8, GWT 2.x baseline, and Jakarta / Jetty 12 migration work, but the post-Jakarta modernization backlog is still open.

## Goal
Finish the remaining modernization work so the project has one consistent build/config story, a complete MongoDB v4 migration path, verified packaging, and a clear post-GWT roadmap.

## Scope
- Config hygiene and flag consolidation.
- MongoDB v4 delta-store completion.
- Remaining library upgrade work.
- SBT parity and packaging / DX verification.
- J2CL / GWT 3 inventory and planning.

## DoD
- The remaining tasks in modernization phases 6 through 8 are completed or explicitly re-scoped.
- The detailed ledger in `docs/modernization-plan.md` matches the live state of the codebase.
- Follow-on product work can proceed without re-opening the infrastructure baseline.
acceptance_criteria:
- No fragment or segment configuration depends on ad-hoc `System.getProperty(...)` paths.
- MongoDB driver v4 path includes `Mongo4DeltaStore`.
- SBT docs and behavior match the actual additive build.
- Packaging and smoke docs reflect the Jakarta default.
- A J2CL / GWT 3 inventory exists.

title: Consolidate config hygiene and fragment flag paths
description: Audit and remove direct server-side `System.getProperty(...)` usage for fragment, segment-state, and related config keys; keep Typesafe Config as the single source of truth with system-property override precedence.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Worktree: /Users/vega/devroot/worktrees/incubator-wave/config-hygiene
Branch: config-hygiene
Lead: Codex team lead
Status: intake started; architect and planner dispatch pending.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Architect summary:
- Root cause: fragment/segment-state config drift caused by mixing injected Config with ConfigFactory.load() and System.getProperty(...) in server paths.
- Likely seams: StatuszServlet.writeFragments, WaveClientServlet.getClientFlags, ServerMain fragment transport/property mirroring.
- Plan file: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-18-config-hygiene-plan.md
- Next: Claude plan review, then worker TDD implementation.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Implementation commit: 5c1388d9

Implementation summary:
- Removed fragment transport/property mirroring from ServerMain.
- Made StatuszServlet read the injected Config directly for transport and segment-state values.
- Reworked WaveClientServlet to derive fragment-related defaults from Config instead of ambient wave.clientFlags system properties.
- Added focused regressions for status config precedence, client fragment defaults, and removal of the transport mirror helper.

Focused verification:
./gradlew -q :wave:compileJava; javac + JUnitCore on StatuszServletConfigTest, WaveClientServletFragmentDefaultsTest, ServerMainFragmentConfigMirroringTest

Residual risk:
- The wider :wave:test task still has unrelated pre-existing test compilation failures outside this change set, so verification was limited to the touched tests and compileJava.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Follow-up implementation commits:
- 0864294c Align config hygiene with Jakarta runtime
- e8e1170a Add config flag precedence regressions

What changed:
- Moved the focused verification to wave/src/jakarta-test so the task is validated on the maintained Jakarta lane rather than the broken legacy compileTestJava lane.
- Brought the active Jakarta WaveClientServlet override into parity with the intended fragment flag precedence: request params, explicit wave.clientFlags overrides, object defaults, derived fragment defaults, then CSV defaults.
- Added focused precedence regressions for object defaults beating derived fragment defaults and CSV defaults still applying when object defaults are absent.

Focused verification:
./gradlew -q :wave:testJakarta --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.rpc.WaveClientServletFragmentDefaultsTest --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.stat.StatuszServletConfigTest

Claude review:
- No blockers.
- Review flagged missing object-form / CSV / precedence coverage; addressed in e8e1170a.
- Review also noted the remaining structural divergence between the legacy and Jakarta WaveClientServlet implementations. That is recorded as a non-blocking follow-up because a deeper deduplication refactor is outside this task scope.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text: PR: https://github.com/vega113/incubator-wave/pull/2

close_reason: Closed after aligning fragment flag resolution with the active Jakarta runtime and verifying focused Jakarta regressions

title: Implement Mongo4DeltaStore and complete the v4 store set

description: Keep Gradle as the canonical build while bringing the SBT port docs, task wiring, and artifact naming into line with the current server-only additive build.

title: Verify packaging, DX, and post-Jakarta smoke guidance
description: Run and document the post-Jakarta packaging and developer-experience pass: distribution builds, smoke scripts, and the developer/operator guidance that now assumes Jakarta by default.

title: Produce the J2CL / GWT 3 inventory and decision memo
description: Inventory GWT-specific surfaces, identify the top blockers for J2CL migration, and record the result as a planning artifact instead of guessing later.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Worktree: /Users/vega/devroot/worktrees/incubator-wave/j2cl-inventory
Branch: j2cl-inventory
Lead: Codex team lead (TL-j2cl-inventory)

Architect summary:
- The codebase still has a large GWT-specific surface: 163 .gwt.xml files, 57 GWT.create callsites, 59 JSNI/JavaScriptObject-heavy files, 232 JSNI native methods, 30 UiBinder-related Java files, and 26 .ui.xml templates.
- There is no JsInterop/Elemental2 bridge layer, while guava-gwt and GWT-specific build/test tasks remain active.
- The main blockers cluster around JSNI/browser interop, UiBinder/widget construction, deferred binding/module sprawl, hosted GWT test infrastructure, and client dependency cleanup.

Plan file: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-18-j2cl-inventory-plan.md
Next: Claude plan review, then planner/worker documentation deliverables.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Plan commit: 18fb650f Plan J2CL inventory task

Claude plan review: pass, no blockers.
Addressed plan-review comments in the plan before worker start:
- made the architect counts explicitly a snapshot that must be re-verified during execution
- removed build.sbt from the evidence list because it is not part of the inventory execution steps
- clarified that follow-on backlog output is proposed task titles in docs/Beads comments, not automatic live Beads issue creation
- added docs/current-state.md to the required ledger updates
- added fd fallbacks for file-count steps.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Implementation commit: 08b75c86 Document J2CL inventory and decision memo

Artifacts:
- docs/j2cl-gwt3-inventory.md
- docs/j2cl-gwt3-decision-memo.md

Ledger updates:
- docs/current-state.md
- docs/modernization-plan.md

Focused verification:
rg -n "J2CL|GWT 3|guava-gwt|UiBinder|JavaScriptObject|JSNI|GWTTestCase" docs/j2cl-gwt3-inventory.md docs/j2cl-gwt3-decision-memo.md docs/modernization-plan.md docs/current-state.md

Claude implementation review:
- Pass, no blockers.
- Non-blocking notes: Beads CLI timestamp parsing bug, optional future expansion of GWT-RPC / @GwtIncompatible counts, and eventual creation of the proposed follow-on tasks before that work begins.

Proposed follow-on task titles:
1. Module graph reduction for the web client
2. Client dependency cleanup
3. JsInterop / Elemental2 bridge pilot
4. JSNI / JavaScriptObject elimination in one vertical slice
5. GWT test harness replacement strategy
6. UiBinder replacement strategy

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text: PR: https://github.com/vega113/incubator-wave/pull/4

title: Honor Jakarta websocket timeout and message-size config
description: Apply `network.websocket_max_idle_time` and `network.websocket_max_message_size` to the Jetty 12 / Jakarta websocket container so the runtime matches the documented config instead of falling back to the container's 30s idle timeout.
close_reason: Fixed by wiring the Jakarta websocket container to the existing timeout and message-size config

title: Harden browser websocket reconnect handling after disconnects
description: Review and harden the browser websocket reconnect loop so later disconnects do not silently exhaust the reconnect budget or leave the client permanently disconnected.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Worktree: /Users/vega/devroot/worktrees/incubator-wave/websocket-reconnect
Branch: websocket-reconnect
Lead: Codex team lead (TL-websocket-reconnect)

Opening task intake:
- Task confirmed ready: blocker incubator-wave-modernization.7 is closed.
- Following docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-18-agent-orchestration-plan.md and docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-18-outstanding-task-batching-plan.md.
- Next: architect root-cause analysis, planner plan update, Claude plan review, worker TDD implementation, reviewer pass, PR to main.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Architect summary:
- Root cause: WaveWebSocketClient spends reconnect budget while already connected and never resets/re-arms the reconnect loop after later disconnects.
- Likely seams: WaveWebSocketClient.connect, reconnectCommand.execute, onConnect, onDisconnect.
- Plan file: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-18-websocket-reconnect-plan.md
- Next: Claude plan review, then worker TDD implementation.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Implementation and review-response commits:
- ad1e865c Harden websocket reconnect loop
- 0995cf41 Move websocket reconnect test to Jakarta lane
- 1e3d4bbd Add reconnect edge-case regressions

What changed:
- The reconnect loop now spends budget only on real connect attempts, resets reconnect state on successful connect, and re-arms reconnect scheduling after disconnect.
- Focused verification was moved onto wave/src/jakarta-test so it runs on the maintained Jakarta lane.
- Added regressions for CONNECTING no-op ticks and budget exhaustion stopping the reconnect loop.

Focused verification:
./gradlew -q :wave:testJakarta --tests org.waveprotocol.box.webclient.client.WaveWebSocketClientTest

Claude review:
- No blockers.
- Review requested explicit coverage for CONNECTING-state ticks and budget exhaustion; addressed in 1e3d4bbd.
- Remaining notes are non-blocking: the CONNECTING branch relies on the underlying transport timeout to bound a stuck in-flight attempt, and the final test lives under jakarta-test rather than src/test because that is the maintained verification lane.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text: PR: https://github.com/vega113/incubator-wave/pull/3

close_reason: Closed after hardening the reconnect loop and adding focused Jakarta regressions for reconnect edge cases

title: Epic: Finish the imported Wiab renderer and fragments work
description:
## Issue
The main `incubator-wave` tree already contains most of the Wiab renderer, quasi-deletion, and fragments scaffolding, but it has not yet been fully validated or finished.

## Goal
Close the remaining gaps in the already-imported Wiab core work so the dynamic renderer and fragments path are clearly supported and testable.

## Scope
- Combined feature verification.
- Remaining public renderer entrypoints.
- Fragment transport and applier behavior.
- Blocks / segment-state follow-up decisions.

## DoD
- The combined renderer + fragments + quasi-deletion path is verified on the current branch.
- Remaining TODO-level code paths are resolved or explicitly documented.
- The canonical docs and Beads backlog accurately describe the supported transport path.
acceptance_criteria:
- Dynamic renderer, fragments, and quasi-deletion can be exercised together without relying on stale merge notes.
- `DynamicRendererImpl` no longer exposes placeholder entrypoints.
- The HTTP-vs-stream fragment path is explicitly supported and documented.
- The follow-up relationship between fragment fetch and deeper blocks adoption is documented.

title: Run combined smoke verification for dynamic renderer, fragments, and quasi deletion
description: Run the merged feature stack together and record the verified behavior so future work does not restart from assumptions.

title: Finish DynamicRenderer public entrypoints and listener validation
description: Replace the remaining `dynamicRendering(...)` TODO bodies with real behavior and add the narrow validation needed to keep the public renderer contract honest.

title: Decide and implement the canonical fragment transport path
description: Either make HTTP fragment mode fully parse and apply payloads, or explicitly reduce it to fallback behavior and declare stream mode canonical.

title: Revisit snapshot gating and storage-backed segment-state follow-ups
description: Re-open the deferred decision around snapshot gating, storage-backed segment state, and the remaining blocks-adoption work now that the merged renderer path is documented.

title: Persist read state when opening a wave from the inbox
description: Ensure that opening an existing wave from the inbox focuses a concrete blip so the normal read-state write path runs and unread counts survive reloads.
close_reason: Fixed by focusing a default blip for plain wave refs and covering the selector path with a regression test

title: Fix unread counts for unopened shared waves
description:
## Issue
When one participant adds a new blip to a shared wave, another participant may not see the unread count reflected in the left messages panel until the wave is opened. That makes unopened-wave digests incorrect and hides unread work.

## Goal
Make the unopened left-panel digest compute unread counts correctly for shared waves, using the same persisted read-state model that should survive reloads.

## Plan
1. Verify the shared-wave unread reproduction against the server-side search/digest path.
2. Add a regression test around the search/digest flow for shared waves with persisted user-data read state.
3. Fix the narrowest root cause in the unopened-wave unread pipeline.
4. Re-run focused tests and document the result in Beads comments.

## DoD
- Shared waves show correct unread counts in the left panel before being opened.
- Reload preserves the same unread result.
- A regression test covers the server/client path chosen for the fix.
close_reason: Fixed in f8a34aab and covered by focused regression tests

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Plan prepared in docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-18-unopened-shared-wave-unread-plan.md

Plan commit: d1f05e4e Plan unopened shared-wave unread fix

Architect finding: unopened-wave unread digests were identifying the viewer user-data wavelet by creator equality instead of user-data wavelet id ownership.

Claude plan review was attempted twice and blocked by provider overload (HTTP 529), not by a technical finding in the plan. Proceeded with the architect + planner outputs.

comments.author: Yuri Z.
comments.text:
Implementation commit: f8a34aab Fix unopened shared-wave unread counts

Implementation summary:
- Fixed unopened-wave unread digest logic to identify the viewer user-data wavelet by wavelet-id ownership instead of creator equality.
- Added regression coverage in wave/src/jakarta-test/java/org/waveprotocol/box/server/waveserver/UnreadSharedWaveDigestTest.java, including a digest-level unread-count assertion.

Focused verification:
./gradlew -q :wave:testJakarta --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.waveserver.UnreadSharedWaveDigestTest --tests org.waveprotocol.wave.client.wavepanel.impl.focus.FocusBlipSelectorTest --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.rpc.ServerRpcProviderWebSocketConfigTest --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.jakarta.WaveWebSocketEndpointInitGuardTest

Review notes:
- Claude implementation review reported no material findings.
- Minor nits only: jakarta-test location, JUnit3 style, and optional extra negative/null UDW coverage.
- Addressed the main coverage concern by adding a digest-level unread-count regression, not just helper-level assertions.

title: Epic: Evaluate and import remaining Wiab-only product features
description:
## Issue
A large part of the core renderer and transport work was imported, but several recognizable Wiab product features never made it into the active Apache Wave tree.

## Goal
Decide which of the remaining Wiab features should be imported, in what order, and under what acceptance criteria.

## Scope
- Tags, archive, and stored searches.
- Draft mode.
- Contacts.
- The deeper snapshot / history / blocks data layer.

## DoD
- The remaining Wiab-only features are either scheduled with explicit tasks or intentionally rejected with written rationale.
- Product-facing imports happen after the underlying core renderer / fragments path is verified.
acceptance_criteria:
- Each major remaining Wiab feature has an explicit audit and go/no-go decision.
- Product features do not reopen infrastructure assumptions that should be solved in the core epic.
- The backlog makes the difference between product UX imports and deep data-layer imports explicit.

title: Audit tags, archive, and stored-search features for import
description: Compare the remaining user-facing tags/archive/search functionality in `Wiab.pro` against the current Apache Wave tree and define the minimum viable import sequence.

title: Evaluate and port draft mode
description: Assess the Wiab draft-mode workflow and determine whether it can be imported cleanly into the current editor and wavepanel stack.

title: Evaluate and port contacts storage and UI
description: Assess the Wiab contacts feature set end-to-end, including stores, RPC endpoints, and UI integration, before deciding on the import scope.

title: Make a go/no-go decision on the deeper Wiab data layer
description: Decide whether the snapshot/history/blocks storage model from `Wiab.pro` should be adopted, partially mined for ideas, or explicitly left out of scope.

title: Fix unread digests for unopened multi-wavelet shared waves
description: When a participant adds a new blip to a shared wave, the recipient can see the unread blip after opening the wave, but the unopened left inbox panel may still show the wave as fully read. Fix the unopened digest path so login-time and unopened-wave unread counts match the opened-wave state.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Worktree: /Users/vega/devroot/worktrees/incubator-wave/unread-panel-live-update
Branch: unread-panel-live-update
Lead: Codex team lead

Initial investigation:
- Repro: unopened inbox row shows no unread state for a shared wave, but opening the wave shows one unread blip.
- Current strongest hypothesis: server-side WaveDigester still counts unread state from only one conversational wavelet, while the opened wave uses the full wave model.
- The repeated `WebSocket session not open` send failures look like stale-session noise unless a focused regression proves they affect persisted unread state.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-19-unread-panel-live-update-plan.md

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Architect review:
- Root cause is the server-side digest path, not websocket reconnect handling.
- Login-time inbox rows come from /search and static digest snapshots; opening the wave switches the row to the live WaveBasedDigest built from the full opened wave.
- The repeated `WebSocket session not open` warnings were investigated and treated as stale-session noise for this task, not as the unread-count root cause.

Implementation commit: 2347ac79 Fix unread digests for unopened multi-wavelet waves

Implementation summary:
- WaveDigester now keeps title/snippet behavior anchored to the main conversation but counts unread and total blips across every conversational wavelet in the wave view.
- Added a regression that reproduces the unopened unread mismatch when the unread blip lives on a secondary conversational wavelet.

Focused verification:
- ./gradlew -q :wave:testJakarta --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.waveserver.UnreadSharedWaveDigestTest --tests org.waveprotocol.wave.client.wavepanel.impl.focus.FocusBlipSelectorTest

comments.author: Codex
comments.text: PR: https://github.com/vega113/incubator-wave/pull/7

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Recovery note:
- Commit bd383709 preserved the PR link but unintentionally reverted WaveDigester.java.
- Restored the WaveDigester fix on top of the existing branch state instead of rewriting history, so the task-history and PR-link comments stay intact.
- Also fixed the WaveDigesterTest call sites for the new generateDigest signature and tightened the new regression to assert the root title remains canonical.

Focused verification after recovery:
- ./gradlew -q :wave:testJakarta --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.waveserver.UnreadSharedWaveDigestTest --tests org.waveprotocol.wave.client.wavepanel.impl.focus.FocusBlipSelectorTest
- ./gradlew -q :wave:test --tests org.waveprotocol.box.server.waveserver.WaveDigesterTest (still blocked by unrelated pre-existing compileTestJava failures outside this task, but the WaveDigesterTest signature mismatch from this change is addressed)

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Merged: https://github.com/vega113/incubator-wave/pull/7

Post-merge verification:
- Fresh vega login against the unread-fix server returned /search?query=in:inbox with wave local.net/w+Bcmv6r8hC7A at unread count 1 before any open request.
- This matches the left-panel data source and closes the original user-visible regression.

Notes:
- CodeRabbit review finished with non-blocking nits only.
- The stale `WebSocket session not open` warnings were investigated and not required for the unread fix path.

close_reason: Merged in PR #7 after restoring server-side unread digest aggregation across conversational wavelets and verifying the login-time inbox search path.

title: Retire the legacy javax fallback profile
description: Remove the opt-in Jetty 9.4 / javax fallback profile and clean the repo down to one supported Jakarta-only server/runtime path.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Worktree: /Users/vega/devroot/worktrees/incubator-wave/retire-javax-fallback
Branch: retire-javax-fallback
Lead: Codex team lead

Decision:
- Product/dev direction is to retire the legacy `jettyFamily=javax` fallback instead of restoring it.
- Rationale: the fallback is currently broken, active work is already gated on the Jakarta path, and keeping the dead profile increases code/build/test surface without current product value.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-19-retire-javax-fallback-plan.md

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Claude plan review round 1:
- Added docs/CONFIG_FLAGS.md and .github/workflows/build.yml to the plan scope.
- Made the fate of jettyFamily and jettyVersion explicit plan decisions instead of implicit assumptions.
- Added the GWT resolutionStrategy audit to the plan so Jetty 9.4 pinning is not left behind.
- Enumerated the known javax-dependent test files that must be triaged during compileTestJava cleanup.
- Tightened verification to include compileTestJava and the Jakarta-only testJakartaIT command, with an optional explicit failure check for -PjettyFamily=javax after retirement.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Review follow-up after round 2 pass-with-nits:
- Added an explicit stale-reference search to Task 4 so the retirement work checks CI, docs, and analysis config for leftover jettyFamily=javax or Jetty 9.4 references.
- Clarified the preferred outcome for the GWT Jetty pin audit: remove the old 9.4 pinning entirely if it only exists to preserve the retired fallback.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Review round 3 result:
- Final external review returned pass with no remaining actionable comments.
- The plan is now considered review-clean and ready for implementation on this branch.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Implementation slice 1:
- Removed the documented/CI Docker contract for the legacy javax fallback and aligned README, current-state, CONFIG_FLAGS, jetty-migration, and modernization docs to Jakarta-only guidance.
- Simplified wave/build.gradle to a Jakarta-only server/runtime profile: removed the live jettyFamily branching, kept the Jetty 9.4 pin only as a GWT-tooling setting (`gwtJettyVersion`), and made the Jakarta source selection unconditional.
- Updated ServerMain so the fallback default no longer silently resolves to javax when no property is present.

Focused verification:
- ./gradlew -q :wave:compileJava
- ./gradlew -q :wave:testJakarta
- ./gradlew -q :wave:testJakartaIT
- ./gradlew -q :wave:compileTestJava

Results:
- compileJava: pass
- testJakarta: pass
- testJakartaIT: pass
- compileTestJava: still blocked by the known legacy test set (FragmentsHttpGatingTest, ServerMainApplierConfigValidationTest, ServerMainConfigValidationTest, DataApiOAuthServletTest, FragmentsServletViewportTest, and related old SessionHandler assumptions).

Review note:
- The plan review loop is complete and clean; no remaining actionable comments on the plan before implementation.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Implementation slice 2:
- Deleted dead fallback-only tests: FragmentsHttpGatingTest, ServerMainApplierConfigValidationTest, ServerMainConfigValidationTest, and JakartaSessionAdaptersTest.
- Deleted the unused JakartaSessionAdapters helper and removed the stale comment reference from the Jakarta AuthenticationServlet override.
- Ported still-relevant tests to the current Jakarta-facing types instead of dropping them: FragmentsServletViewportTest and DataApiOAuthServletTest.

Focused verification:
- ./gradlew -q :wave:compileTestJava
- ./gradlew -q :wave:compileJava
- ./gradlew -q :wave:testJakarta
- ./gradlew -q :wave:testJakartaIT

Results:
- compileTestJava: pass
- compileJava: pass
- testJakarta: pass
- testJakartaIT: pass

External review:
- Claude-compatible fallback review pass completed on this exact diff with no blockers and no required follow-ups. The review agreed that dead fallback tests were removed rather than masked, and that the remaining relevant tests were ported instead of dropped.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Implementation slice 3:
- Removed the remaining no-op runtime/config fallback knob from the active server path by deleting the `core.jetty_family` config entry and simplifying ServerMain to Jakarta-only behavior.
- Removed the last active build-time `-DjettyFamily=jakarta` injection because the build/runtime path is now inherently Jakarta-only.
- Cleaned one active doc reference in blocks-adoption-plan that still pointed at the deleted FragmentsHttpGatingTest fallback test.

Focused verification:
- ./gradlew -q :wave:compileJava
- ./gradlew -q :wave:testJakarta
- ./gradlew -q :wave:testJakartaIT
- ./gradlew -q :wave:compileTestJava
- rg stale-reference sweep over README/docs/.github/Dockerfile/wave/build.gradle/wave/config/wave/src/main/java/wave/src/test/java

Results:
- All four Gradle verification commands passed.
- Remaining `jettyFamily` / deleted-test references are limited to historical ledgers and the retirement plan itself, not live build/runtime config.

comments.text:
Implementation summary:
- f3299771 Retire the javax fallback build profile
  - Removed the documented/CI/Docker fallback contract and made the Gradle server/runtime path Jakarta-only.
- 2903737f Remove dead javax fallback tests
  - Deleted dead fallback-only tests and the unused JakartaSessionAdapters helper, while porting still-relevant tests to the current Jakarta-facing types.
- 67a28ffe Remove residual javax fallback knobs
  - Removed the remaining no-op runtime/config knob (`core.jetty_family`, `-DjettyFamily=jakarta`) and cleaned the last active doc reference to a deleted fallback test.

Verification:
- ./gradlew -q :wave:compileJava
- ./gradlew -q :wave:compileTestJava
- ./gradlew -q :wave:testJakarta
- ./gradlew -q :wave:testJakartaIT
- ./gradlew -q :wave:smokeInstalled
- ./gradlew :wave:run (verified 302 redirect at / on the retire branch server)
- stale-reference grep across README/docs/.github/Dockerfile/wave/build.gradle/wave/config/wave/src/main/java/wave/src/test/java

External review:
- Plan review loop completed cleanly (final round pass with no actionable comments).
- Full branch external review passed with no blockers and only one non-blocking archival nit about the plan doc.

Result:
- The repo now has one supported Jakarta-only server/runtime path on this branch.
- Remaining references to the old fallback are limited to historical ledger text and the retirement plan itself, not live build/runtime config.
